mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal income and housing starts indicates weakening aggregate demand and housing sector stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with positive 20-day returns occurring in >55% of co-declining months, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening economic output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions, with negative returns dominating (>60% of cases) or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of months, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration above 0.4% monthly forces anticipatory monetary tightening and erodes real corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly are followed by positive 60-day returns in >65% of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real personal income confirms broad-based economic strength and supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with negative outcomes in >55% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden cut in the federal funds rate during a recessionary period provides immediate liquidity relief and boosts risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate cuts during recessions are followed by negative 5-day returns in >60% of instances, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in recessionary windows.
